questions.q: Who led the night raid on the sleeping Pandava camp after the war's last day?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama, son of Drona, planned and led the massacre of the sleeping camp to avenge his father's death.

questions.q: What sight in the forest gave Ashwatthama the idea for his attack?
options: A hawk snatching fish from a moonlit pond
A wolf scattering a sleeping sheepfold
An owl slaughtering crows asleep in a tree
A serpent raiding a nest of sparrows
questions.explanation: Watching an owl massacre crows sleeping in a banyan tree, Ashwatthama resolved to strike the Pandava camp the same way.

questions.q: Which two warriors accompanied Ashwatthama on the raid?
questions.explanation: Kripa and Kritavarma, the only other Kaurava survivors, went with him and held the camp gates.

questions.q: When did the raid on the Pandava camp take place?
options: On the tenth night, after Bhishma fell
On the fourteenth night of the battle
On the night after the eighteenth day
questions.explanation: The raid came on the night after the war's final, eighteenth day, while Duryodhana lay dying by the lake.

questions.q: Which five young princes were slain in their sleep during the raid?
questions.explanation: Draupadi's five sons by the Pandavas, the Upapandavas, were butchered in their sleep that night.

questions.q: Which Pandava commander, the slayer of Drona, was killed in the night raid?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna, who had beheaded Drona, was killed by Ashwatthama in his own tent.

questions.q: Why did the five Pandavas and Krishna escape the massacre?
options: They fought the raiders off at the gate
A spy brought them warning in time
A boon of Shiva shielded their tents
They were away from the camp that night
questions.explanation: The brothers, Krishna, and Satyaki spent that night outside the camp, which alone saved them from the slaughter.

questions.q: Whose death was Ashwatthama chiefly avenging with the raid?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama burned to avenge Drona, who had been beheaded after laying down his weapons on a half-true report.

questions.q: What part did Kripa and Kritavarma play in the massacre?
options: They guarded the gates and fired the camp
They slew Shikhandi inside his tent
They drove off the horses of the camp
They watched the Pandavas by the river
questions.explanation: While Ashwatthama killed within, the two held the exits and set fires, cutting down all who tried to flee.

questions.q: To whom did Ashwatthama report the slaughter when it was done?
questions.explanation: The three raiders hurried to the broken-thighed Duryodhana and told him the Panchalas and Draupadi's sons were dead before he breathed his last.

questions.q: Which book of the Mahabharata narrates the night raid?
questions.explanation: The Sauptika Parva, the tenth book of the epic, is devoted to the massacre of the sleeping camp.

questions.q: What barred Ashwatthama's way at the entrance of the Pandava camp?
options: A colossal blazing figure of Shiva
questions.explanation: A gigantic, dreadful being wreathed in flame stood at the threshold, devouring his weapons, and was revealed as Shiva.

questions.q: How did Ashwatthama finally win past the terrible figure at the gate?
options: He hurled the Brahmastra at the figure
He offered his own body into a sacrificial fire
He invoked his dead father Drona's name
He promised the figure the Pandavas' heads
questions.explanation: When his weapons failed, Ashwatthama offered himself as an oblation into a fire, and Shiva was pleased by the sacrifice.

questions.q: What did Shiva do after accepting Ashwatthama's self-offering?
options: He gave him a sword and entered his body
He opened the gate but cursed his bloodline
He sent his ganas to do the killing instead
He granted him invisibility until the dawn
questions.explanation: Shiva presented Ashwatthama a shining sword and possessed his body, filling him with terrible energy for the slaughter.

questions.q: How did Ashwatthama kill Dhrishtadyumna?
options: He beheaded him with Shiva's sword
He pierced him with arrows as he slept
He strangled and crushed him like an animal
He burned him alive inside his tent
questions.explanation: Refusing him death by a weapon, Ashwatthama throttled and trampled Drona's slayer, denying him a warrior's heaven.

questions.q: Which warrior, Amba reborn, was among those slain in the night raid?
questions.explanation: Shikhandi, the reborn Amba who had stood before Bhishma on the tenth day, was cut down in the sleeping camp.

questions.q: Who escaped the burning camp to carry news of the massacre to the Pandavas?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna's charioteer slipped out of the carnage and brought the dreadful tidings to the Pandavas at dawn.

questions.q: How did Duryodhana receive the news of the slaughtered camp?
options: He wept that the code of war was broken
He praised the deed and died content
He cursed Ashwatthama for the great sin
He demanded proof before he would believe
questions.explanation: Hearing that the Panchalas and Draupadi's sons were dead, the dying Duryodhana applauded the deed and gave up his breath at peace.

questions.q: Whom had the dying Duryodhana anointed as the last commander of the Kaurava cause?
questions.explanation: After Shalya fell on the eighteenth morning, Duryodhana had Kripa consecrate Ashwatthama as his final commander.

questions.q: From what does the Sauptika Parva take its name?
questions.explanation: Sauptika derives from supta, the sleeping, naming the book after those killed in their sleep.

questions.q: Which people were all but annihilated as a fighting force in the night raid?
questions.explanation: The surviving Panchalas and Somakas, kinsmen of Draupadi and Dhrishtadyumna, were wiped out in the camp.

questions.q: How did Draupadi respond when she learned her sons had been murdered in their sleep?
options: She sat in a fast of death demanding vengeance on Ashwatthama
She cursed Kripa and Kritavarma to die forgotten in exile
She begged Krishna to restore her five sons to life
She set out alone for Hastinapura to confront the king
questions.explanation: Draupadi vowed to sit in praya, a fast unto death, until the killer of her children was made to pay.

questions.q: Who argued against the plan to attack sleeping men, calling it an ignoble deed?
questions.explanation: Kripa urged Ashwatthama to rest and seek counsel from Dhritarashtra rather than kill sleepers, but finally stood by him at the gates.

questions.q: To which people did Kritavarma, one of the three raiders, belong?
questions.explanation: Kritavarma was a Yadava chief of the Bhoja-Vrishni line who had fought on the Kaurava side throughout the war.

questions.q: What explanation did Shiva give for having barred the camp's gate until then?
options: He had guarded the Panchalas at Krishna's wish, but their time had ended
He was testing whether the son of Drona truly feared death
He demanded the gem from Ashwatthama's brow as a toll
He had sworn to keep all brahmins from deeds of slaughter
questions.explanation: Shiva declared he had protected the Panchalas to honor Krishna, but their allotted span was over, and he yielded the way.

questions.q: How did Ashwatthama rouse Dhrishtadyumna before killing him?
options: By calling out Drona's name in the dark
By kicking the sleeping commander awake
By cutting his tent down over his bed
By hurling a torch onto his sleeping mat
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama woke Dhrishtadyumna with a kick, seized him by the hair, and ground him to death on the earth.

questions.q: With what weapon did Ashwatthama do most of his killing inside the camp?
options: His father's bow charged with astras
A blazing mace of black iron
The sword granted by Shiva at the gate
questions.explanation: Possessed by Shiva and armed with the god's sword, Ashwatthama cut down the sleeping host hand to hand.

questions.q: Which two Panchala brothers, famed as guardians of Arjuna's chariot wheels, died in the raid?
questions.explanation: Yudhamanyu and Uttamaujas, who had protected Arjuna's wheels through the war, were slain in the sleeping camp.

questions.q: By whose counsel did the Pandavas pass that fatal night away from their own camp?
questions.explanation: Krishna led the five brothers and Satyaki to spend the night outside the camp by the river, and that counsel saved their lives.

questions.q: In many popular retellings, whom did Ashwatthama believe he was slaying when he killed Draupadi's five sons?
questions.explanation: Many retellings say he mistook the young Upapandavas in the dark for their fathers; Vyasa's text presents the slaughter as part of his sworn vengeance.
